climate.rainfallSeasonality: Relatively even distribution with a slight autumn-winter peak

primary.description: Melbourne's extensive stormwater infrastructure is regularly overwhelmed during intense rainfall, causing flash flooding in low-lying suburbs along the Maribyrnong River and Elster Creek corridors.
Severe thunderstorms and cold front passages produce destructive wind gusts exceeding 100 km/h, causing widespread roof damage and tree falls across the metropolitan area.
Outer eastern and northern suburbs including Eltham, Warrandyte, and the Dandenong Ranges face direct bushfire threat, with ember attack possible in adjacent residential areas.

secondary.description: Winter temperatures regularly drop below 5°C across Melbourne, causing pipe failures in older properties particularly in the inner west and northern suburbs.
Melbourne experiences significant hailstorm events, with supercell thunderstorms producing hailstones large enough to damage roofing, skylights, and vehicles.

risks.bushfireAttackLevel: BAL-12.5 to BAL-19 for outer eastern suburbs (Eltham, Warrandyte, Hurstbridge fringe)
risks.floodZone: Moderate — Maribyrnong River corridor, Elster Creek catchment, Yarra River floodplain

historicalEvents.description: The deadliest bushfire disaster in Australian history killed 173 people and destroyed over 2,000 homes across Victoria. Peri-urban Melbourne suburbs experienced ember attack, and smoke damage affected properties across the eastern metropolitan area.

A severe thunderstorm system produced destructive winds and heavy rainfall across the metropolitan area, causing widespread roof damage, flooding, and power outages affecting hundreds of thousands of properties.
An unprecedented thunderstorm asthma event overwhelmed emergency services. Associated storm damage from the preceding cold front caused significant property damage across Melbourne's western suburbs.

Prolonged rainfall across Victoria caused major flooding along the Maribyrnong River, inundating homes in Maribyrnong and Flemington. The Yarra River reached minor flood level, with hundreds of properties requiring water damage restoration.

suburbs.name: Footscray
suburbs.riskNote: Flash flooding risk from Maribyrnong River

suburbs.name: Williamstown
suburbs.riskNote: Coastal exposure and storm surge risk

suburbs.name: Maribyrnong
suburbs.riskNote: High flood risk — Maribyrnong River corridor

suburbs.name: Eltham
suburbs.riskNote: Bushfire risk — BAL-12.5 to BAL-19 zone

council.name: City of Melbourne
council.sesUnit: Victoria SES Metropolitan West / Metropolitan East

serviceNotes.waterDamage: Melbourne's ageing stormwater infrastructure and clay soils make water ingress a persistent problem, particularly in period homes across the inner suburbs. Rapid water extraction and structural drying are critical to prevent subfloor damage in these properties.
serviceNotes.fireDamage: Smoke and soot damage from bushfire events can affect properties well beyond the fire front. Melbourne's eastern suburbs regularly require smoke damage assessment and remediation following fire activity in the Dandenong Ranges and Yarra Valley corridors.
serviceNotes.mouldRemediation: Melbourne's cool, damp winters combined with poor ventilation in older buildings create ideal conditions for mould colonisation. Properties along creek corridors and in flood-prone areas require proactive moisture management.
serviceNotes.stormDamage: Destructive wind gusts from severe thunderstorms and cold front passages cause significant roof damage across Melbourne. Rapid emergency tarping and make-safe services prevent secondary water damage from rain ingress.
